### Remaining works

Plan for Figures.

- Fig1. For the method part (Already decided)

 - A. The overview workflow of Tribus + Napari

 - B. A use case of Tribus in the imaging analysis pipeline

 - C. An example of cell type logic table (from level 1 to 2)

- Fig2. Tribus applied to published datasets. (CODEX and MIBI-TOF)

 - A. tiff images with channels; origin cell type label; labeled by
 > Tribus

 - B. Metrics barplot per cell type \[2 datasets\]

 - CODEX has 8 regions, we can calculate error bar?

 - MIBI-TOF has 41 samples so we can calculate metrics per
 > sample and add the error bar on the barplot

 - C. Heatmap expression \[2 datasets\]

 - D. UMAP \[2 datasets\]

- Fig3. Compare with other cell-type classification methods.

 - A. Number of annotated cells

 - B. Barplot of overall metrics

 - C. Barplot of metrics per cell type

```{=html}
<!-- -->
```
- Fig4. Tribus on Pre&Post dataset

 - A. tiff images with channels; labeled by Tribus

 - B. Heatmap expression & UMAP

- Fig5. Tribus on TMA

 - A. tiff images with channels; labeled by Tribus

 - B. Highlight the lymphoid cells that are corrected by Tribus

Ziqi:

- Will finish the scripts to generate benchmarking figures

 - If we adjust the logic table and update the result, all the
 > figures could be generated again easily

- Will try my best to work on manuscript before Aug 15th

rking figures

 - If we adjust the logic table and update the result, all the
 > figures could be generated again easily

- Will try my best to work on manuscript before Aug 15th

 - After Aug 15th I need to use 50% of my time to finish the
 > doctoral program application

```{=html}
<!-- -->
```
- Problem when benchmarking:

```{=html}
<!-- -->
```
- Tribus is not stable on the CODEX dataset.

 - Currently, we set.seed to make sure we get the same output, but
 > the result might crash if we change seeds.

 - The SOM algorithm itself includes randomization - maybe we can't
 > overcome this non-stable problem.

- Need further adjustment on the MIBI-TOF dataset logic table

- Need to decide which classic clustering method to compare with.
 > PhenoGraph or ...?

### List of similar works

ACDC:

### Question for meeting with Anniina 

- For manuscript

 - Is the current structure ok?

[https://doi.org/10.1093/bib/bbad260]{.underline}](https://doi.org/10.1093/bib/bbad260)

### Question for meeting with Anniina 

- For manuscript

 - Is the current structure ok?

 - **Should we compare Tribus with other classical clustering
 > methods used for cell type classification? For example,
 > Phenograph?**

 - Should we introduce tCyCIF in the method section?

- How to put the tutorial online?

- Tribus is not stable / not as accurate as we hoped

 - 1\. We could continue to finish the manuscript, it might require
 > major revision but we will have the direction to work on with

 - 2\. Consider replacing SOM with other algorithms - this means a
 > big change on Tribus

Meeting notes:

- Put the text in GoogleDocs for Anniina for editing → Also for
 > submission they will ask separately!! (text + figures) Probably a
 > Word document will be needed, although in Briefings in
 > bioinformatics it can be submitted in LaTEX but we can still
 > decide to submit somewhere else

- Submission probably earliest in end of September, but realistically
 > later

- TMA normalize patient by patient (staining dependent on how the
 > blocks were fixed)

- Still ask for help for logic table

- Sometimes the expression patterns are strange (real cells expressing
 > unexpected patterns/combinations) → Take a picture of the cells,

)

- Still ask for help for logic table

- Sometimes the expression patterns are strange (real cells expressing
 > unexpected patterns/combinations) → Take a picture of the cells,
 > show then that they are true → We can report in the biological
 > part of the results

- For manual clustering we can compare it with the TMA data → it was
 > done by clustering and then manual labeling, we do not need
 > another one, only if the reviewers ask

- Adding tCyCIF to methods because our data done by this method

- Also describe the dataset (pre&post data) for supplementary
 > material, overview

- Synaps folder for data, the link for the tutorial can also go there

- Hosing the tutorial on Read the docs (open source is free)

- Diagnose how and where it is not so stable and accurate (also list
 > for ourselves where it does not work as good as hoped)

- At least having classification on a few levels, and going deeper by
 > gating (where eg few markers etc)

- The performance of Tribus depends on the image quality, signal to
 > noise ratio (can we find a cutoff?)

- Identify what are the celltypes/conditions which are not performing
 > well

- Being open and honest wit examples → These are the situation where
 > you have to do gating because at the moment no algorithm works,

- Ada's images → Showcasing how Tribus works in reduced dimensions

wit examples → These are the situation where
 > you have to do gating because at the moment no algorithm works,

- Ada's images → Showcasing how Tribus works in reduced dimensions
 > (7-8 plexed images) (hopefully really good) → Clinical settings
 > you usually can't do hiperplexed

- Supplementary material: Marker distribution plots to justify when
 > they work or no

- If excluding the patient and works → document it and mention in
 > supplementary what are the potential situation where Tribus gives
 > unexpected/random results

- In methods explain tCyCIF method and highlight the new datasets

- Spatial biology should be from pre&post or Ada's samples, not from
 > TMA (eg highlight CD8T cells and myeloid interaction)

- Focus on finishing results and figures (until Teo is here)

- If we use Ada's data, it can go to the figure with pre and post →
 > biological meaning

- Figures on priority, writing after!
